claim_status: Browser benchmark launch runner contract only; no browser benchmark run, no benchmark result, no trace captured, no raw sample, no memory result, no energy result, no competitor result, no Chrome-class claim, and no performance claim.

required_arguments.name: --hardware-id
required_arguments.purpose: Bind the run to a checked benchmark hardware registry entry.

required_arguments.name: --os-control-id
required_arguments.purpose: Bind the run to a checked OS-control registry entry.

required_arguments.name: --corpus-id
required_arguments.purpose: Select the reviewed offline corpus manifest.

required_arguments.name: --network-profile-id
required_arguments.purpose: Select the local server and network-control profile.

required_arguments.name: --browser-pin-id
required_arguments.purpose: Select owner-reviewed isolated browser-reported version and settings evidence.

required_arguments.name: --tab-scenario-set-id
required_arguments.purpose: Select the 30-tab mixed/all-live workload denominator contract.

required_arguments.name: --artifact-root
required_arguments.purpose: Write all run artifacts to a runner-owned package root outside real user profiles.

required_arguments.name: --claim-mode
required_arguments.purpose: Force no-claim output until owner-reviewed Level 3 claim evidence exists.

command_contract.self_test_status: tools/run_benchmark_browser_launch.py --self-test validates command parsing, forbidden arguments, checked registry references, artifact-root handling, and no-claim finalization while launching no browser and producing no benchmark result.

target_policy.target_resolution: Future real runs must resolve each target from owner-reviewed browser pins, not from ambient PATH lookup or real user profile state.
target_policy.profile_boundary: Future real runs must create runner-owned temporary profiles and must not read or write real user profiles, cookies, history, sync data, extensions, or account state.
target_policy.network_boundary: Future real runs must use checked local network profiles unless a reviewed benchmark explicitly profiles external network behavior.
target_policy.comparison_boundary: Competitor comparison remains blocked until browser pins, command lines, settings, caches, lifecycle, security, and workload are equivalent and reviewed.

stage_contract.name: Validate registry references and no-claim mode
stage_contract.failure_policy: A missing or unreviewed registry blocks the run and writes a failure denominator record.

stage_contract.name: Start checked local server profile
stage_contract.failure_policy: Server startup, route, Host-header, or fixture failure blocks measurement and remains in the denominator.

stage_contract.name: Resolve browser target from reviewed pin
stage_contract.failure_policy: Unreviewed, stale, or mismatched browser pins block comparison and write a failure denominator record.

stage_contract.name: Create runner-owned temporary profile
stage_contract.failure_policy: Any real-profile path or failed cleanup record blocks claim eligibility.

stage_contract.name: Apply cache and storage reset policy
stage_contract.failure_policy: Unknown cache or storage state remains in the denominator and blocks equal-workload comparison.

stage_contract.name: Start trace and counter collectors
stage_contract.failure_policy: Missing trace collection blocks Level 1 or higher performance evidence.

stage_contract.name: Launch browser under test
stage_contract.failure_policy: Launch failures, crashes, and wrong-target launches are recorded as denominator failures.

stage_contract.name: Run warmup sequence
stage_contract.failure_policy: Warmup failures do not disappear; they block or label the run according to the reviewed benchmark plan.

stage_contract.name: Run measured sample sequence
stage_contract.failure_policy: Unsupported, timed-out, discarded, crashed, or cancelled samples remain in the denominator.

stage_contract.name: Finalize artifact package and raw result manifest
stage_contract.failure_policy: Incomplete finalization marks the run invalid for claims while retaining diagnostic artifacts.

stage_contract.name: Cleanup browser, server, traces, and temporary profiles
stage_contract.failure_policy: Cleanup failures are retained and block benchmark-ready status until reviewed.

stage_contract.name: Enforce no-claim result finalization
stage_contract.failure_policy: The runner must fail closed if claim metadata is absent or attempts to promote sample data.

sample_control.warmup_policy: Warmup count, excluded warmup samples, cache state, and ordering must be recorded before measured samples can be interpreted.
sample_control.randomization_policy: Target, scenario, and sample order must be recorded and reviewed before any competitor comparison.
sample_control.denominator_policy: All failed, unsupported, timed-out, cancelled, crashed, discarded, and state-loss cases remain in the denominator.
sample_control.result_label: harness smoke evidence only until owner-reviewed Level 3 claim evidence exists

cache_profile_policy.cookie_storage_policy: No real cookies or account state; generated corpus cookies only when a reviewed benchmark case requires them.
cache_profile_policy.extension_policy: Extensions disabled unless the workload explicitly measures extension overhead and includes equivalent competitor settings.
cache_profile_policy.account_sync_policy: No account or sync attachment in benchmark runs.

resource_attribution_policy.unknown_bucket_policy: Unknown resource buckets must remain visible and block owner-reviewed claims above the configured threshold.
resource_attribution_policy.shared_resource_policy: Shared browser, network, storage, GPU, and agent costs must be charged using the semantic resource-attribution taxonomy.

unsupported_behavior: no browser benchmark launch implementation exists beyond the checked no-browser self-test
no browser benchmark run was executed
no browser was launched by this contract
no trace, raw sample, memory snapshot, power sample, or artifact package was generated by this contract
no timeout, cancellation, cache reset, warmup, sample, failure, cleanup, or result-finalization implementation exists beyond self-test validations

no competitor comparison, browser-ready pin, Chrome-class, faster, lower-memory, lower-energy, daily-driver, production, beta, stable, accessibility, security, compatibility, or performance claim is supported
evidence_required: implemented browser benchmark launch runner beyond the checked no-browser self-test and reviewed command contract
runner-managed local server artifact and DNS/cache/network evidence
owner-reviewed benchmark-ready browser pins with isolated profiles, settings, command lines, and cleanup evidence
trace and artifact package generated by a real run with SHA-256 manifest, redaction review, and retention decision
raw result manifest with sample order, warmup, timeouts, cancellations, failures, unsupported cases, tab lifecycle, process topology, and resource-attribution IDs
negative tests proving forbidden arguments, real profile paths, hidden failures, missing claim metadata, unequal workload settings, and disabled security mitigations fail closed
